statement: The highest-value migration addition is a first-class iteration snapshot artifact that captures cohort status, run summaries, diagnostics, stuck signals, and trace refs before every wipe.
rationale: Current observability is adequate but fragmented; repeated rediscovery risk comes from wiping before preserving a composed forensic bundle.
keyAssumptions: The campaign loop will keep resetting run-scoped state repeatedly.
validationCriteria: A later agent can explain a failed pass from the iteration artifact alone without live DB state.
critiquePoints: A strong cohort-level codex API could reduce the amount of artifact assembly needed.
